topic: Verdict Assignment Criteria — post-patch review of TLDR, passive-voice, false-dilemma, and counting-methodology fixes (EXP-155 applied)
change_summary: Applied fixes from EXP-155 addressing the c1 review of eval-guide-eg-verdicts (ISS-902/903/904/910). Changes: (1) TLDR rewritten to counterfactual-test wording; (2) 'actively mishandle' → passive/structural voice; (3) false-dilemma subtype qualified as compound failure; (4) second worked example added for Self-Contradicted-vs-Misleading priority; (5) new paragraph acknowledging tidal count-inflation symmetry.
our_argument_summary: The patched section defines six verdict categories, articulates a counterfactual primary-issue rule, distinguishes Misleading from Std Model Explains via passive-voice structural-mishandling framing, qualifies false-dilemma framing as Misleading only when compounded with additional mishandling (WIN-054 cited as the pure-false-dilemma reference case → Not Demonstrated), adds a second worked example covering the Self-Contradicted vs Misleading boundary, and preempts the hypocrisy objection by acknowledging the tidal count-inflation symmetry in our own verdict accounting. Absence of a 'Confirmed' category remains transparent with restated add-conditions.

holes_found.description: The count-inflation preemption paragraph uses the range notation '(045–051)' for the five tidal WINs but that range spans SEVEN WIN IDs (045, 046, 047, 048, 049, 050, 051), only five of which are tidal. WIN-047 (Low-z Hubble Law aetheric) and WIN-048 (CMB Axis of Evil) are inside that range but are not tidal — they carry the Misleading verdict for unrelated reasons. A defender reading the criteria document will immediately say 'you claim WIN-047 and WIN-048 are tidal Self-Contradicted WINs — they are neither tidal nor Self-Contradicted.' The earlier inline list is correct ('M2, S2, K1, O1, N2 as five separate predictions') but the subsequent parenthetical '(045–051)' contradicts it. This is exactly the kind of range-notation error the site flags in the dome model.
holes_found.recommendation: Replace '(045–051)' with an explicit list: 'Five tidal WINs (045, 046, 049, 050, 051)'. Alternative: 'five tidal WINs in the 045–051 range'. The explicit-list form is preferred because it doubles as a citation and prevents further drift if the numbering changes.

holes_found.description: Logical overlap in the qualified false-dilemma subtype. The patched text lists three 'additional mishandling' items that keep false-dilemma Misleading rather than Not Demonstrated: '(1) misrepresenting significance, (2) contradicting other dome claims, or (3) lacking any dome-specific derivation.' But the adjacent sentence defines pure false-dilemma — the Not Demonstrated case — as 'correctly identifying a genuine anomaly without offering dome physics.' Item (3) ('lacking any dome-specific derivation') is functionally identical to 'without offering dome physics.' A WIN that exhibits false-dilemma framing AND lacks a dome-specific derivation would satisfy both definitions simultaneously — it would be Misleading by item (3) and Not Demonstrated by the pure case. A dome defender challenging any Misleading-via-false-dilemma WIN can point at item (3) and force reclassification to Not Demonstrated (weaker verdict) or, conversely, challenge WIN-054's ND verdict by invoking item (3).
holes_found.recommendation: Drop item (3) from the 'additional mishandling' list, leaving (1) misrepresenting significance and (2) contradicting other dome claims — both of which are genuinely ADDITIONAL to the bare false-dilemma pattern. If a third compounding factor is desired, consider 'post-hoc adoption of a published value already in standard references' (WIN-047/055 reason) which is a separate structural failure.

holes_found.description: Counterfactual wording drift between TLDR and body paragraph. TLDR: 'the failure that would persist even if the others were corrected takes priority'. Body: 'the verdict that captures the primary structural failure — the one that, if corrected, would still leave the claim unsupported.' The TLDR formulation is clean: keep this issue, fix the others, claim still broken → this issue is primary. The body formulation is ambiguous in isolation: 'if corrected [which issue?], would still leave the claim unsupported [why?].' A literal reading (fix THIS issue alone, claim still unsupported) does not uniquely identify a primary failure — in a multi-failure WIN, correcting any single issue individually leaves the claim unsupported because the others remain. The second worked example (Self-Contradicted vs Misleading) implicitly applies the TLDR form ('even with correctly represented data, the dome's own math still fails'), so the body paragraph is inconsistent with its own example.
holes_found.recommendation: Align body with TLDR: 'the primary structural failure — the one that would persist even if the other failures were corrected.' This matches the TLDR and the worked example's implicit criterion. Minor wording change but removes a logical ambiguity that AI reviewers will flag.

holes_found.description: The preemption paragraph's scope is narrower than the defender's strongest version of the hypocrisy objection. It addresses tidal WINs (5 verdicts from 1 argument) but the dome site's count-inflation pattern is broader: the eclipse WINs (17+ claimed confirmations all resting on the same local-moon + differential-refraction geometry, now discussed in SEC-1.9) and the ELF/Schumann cluster also derive multiple WINs from one underlying claim. A defender will say 'if five tidal Self-Contradicted = one geometric contradiction, then seventeen eclipse Self-Contradicted = one geometric contradiction too; your whole Self-Contradicted count is inflated by the same factor you accuse us of.' The current paragraph discloses the tidal case but not the eclipse case, which is numerically larger.
holes_found.recommendation: Extend the preemption sentence slightly: 'Five tidal WINs (045, 046, 049, 050, 051) each receive Self-Contradicted because the dome's local moon geometry fails independently for each constituent's period; similarly, the eclipse-geometry WINs share the refraction-indeterminacy argument developed in Section 1.9.' This is not essential — the tidal acknowledgment is already a significant credibility gain — but it fully closes the hypocrisy attack surface.

holes_found.description: Hardcoded 'six' still appears four times in the body text. Carried over from c1 review (flagged minor, not patched). If a Confirmed category is ever added, all four references plus the verdict-legend require simultaneous update. Not worth a patch cycle in isolation; noting for completeness in case other build-time count-derivation work is undertaken.
holes_found.recommendation: Defer. Current build does not compute verdict category count from verdict-legend metadata. Mark as 'not worth a patch cycle' in tracker.

stronger_arguments.description: The 'Self-Contradicted: the internal-consistency standard' paragraph could explicitly name the key logical property: Self-Contradicted verdicts are robust under the ad-hoc-rescue defense. A dome defender typically responds to data-based criticism with 'your measurement is wrong' or 'standard physics is wrong.' Self-Contradicted verdicts are immune to both moves because the contradiction is constructed from the dome's own equations and parameters. Making this rescue-immunity explicit would strengthen the section against the broader 'you're biased against us' meta-objection.
sources: Internal: overlaps with part5 kill-shot framing; part3 roadmap references Self-Contradicted as 'terminal' category

stronger_arguments.description: The Misleading vs Std Model Explains decision boundary paragraph would benefit from a worked example analogous to the primary-issue rule's example. Stating the criterion abstractly ('has the evidence been mishandled') leaves room for interpretation; a concrete comparison (e.g., WIN-X → Std Model Explains because cited value is correct and dome adds no derivation vs WIN-Y → Misleading because cited value contradicts the source) would make the boundary auditable.
sources: Internal: WIN-043 (NMP drift) vs WIN-047 (Hubble H0) illustrate this boundary; both adopt standard values but differ in whether additional mishandling is present

kernel_of_truth.description: The patch is a genuine improvement. It resolved all four holes flagged in the c1 review (passive-voice, false-dilemma inconsistency, primary-issue boundary example, tidal count asymmetry) without introducing prose bloat. The counterfactual-test framing in the TLDR is materially more precise than the prior 'most structurally damaging' wording, and the new counting-methodology paragraph is exactly the kind of self-disclosure that defuses the hypocrisy attack before it can be made. The analyst/decider loop here worked as designed.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: No claim is at risk — the section itself is a methodology document, not a WIN. The remaining issues (range-notation error, subtype overlap, TLDR-body wording drift) are cleanup items, not structural defects. The 'major' severity on the range-notation issue reflects its visibility and exploitability as a process objection, not any threat to the overall review.

text_fingerprint.tldr_text: Each WIN receives the verdict matching its primary failure mode. When a WIN fails in multiple ways, the failure that would persist even if the others were corrected takes priority — a counterfactual test, not a severity ranking. The categories describe different kinds of failure, not degrees of it.

tldr_review.tldr_text: Each WIN receives the verdict matching its primary failure mode. When a WIN fails in multiple ways, the failure that would persist even if the others were corrected takes priority — a counterfactual test, not a severity ranking. The categories describe different kinds of failure, not degrees of it.
tldr_review.notes: TLDR now correctly uses counterfactual-test framing and explicitly disclaims severity ranking. Body paragraph's equivalent sentence uses slightly different wording that introduces a logical ambiguity (flagged as moderate hole). The TLDR is fine on its own; the body should be brought into alignment with it.

4 of 5 fully resolved (passive-voice ✓, false-dilemma qualified ✓, 2nd worked example ✓, counting-methodology acknowledgment ✓); TLDR hole partially resolved (TLDR fixed, body paragraph wording drift remains); 'six' hardcode explicitly deferred (c1 minor)

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: Not applicable — this is a methodology section in our review, not a WIN. monitor.py is dome-owned code and irrelevant here.

deeper_analysis:
The EXP-155 patch set is a high-quality response to the c1 review. Four of the five holes were addressed directly and at proportionate length. The counterfactual-test TLDR is materially better than 'most structurally damaging,' and the self-disclosure paragraph on tidal count symmetry is exactly the sort of preemptive candor that neutralizes the hypocrisy attack most effectively. The primary-issue rule's second worked example correctly picks Self-Contradicted over Misleading when both apply — this matters because WIN-012 and others may rest on exactly that boundary.

The residual issues fall into three classes. (1) A factual slip — the '(045–051)' range notation contradicts the inline 'M2/S2/K1/O1/N2' enumeration and will read as carelessness to a sophisticated defender. This is the most exploitable finding because it provides a concrete process objection independent of any substantive argument. (2) A logical overlap in the qualified false-dilemma subtype — listing 'lacking any dome-specific derivation' as an 'additional mishandling' while defining pure false-dilemma as 'without offering dome physics' creates a definitional loop. A defender can use this to challenge Misleading WIN-047/048/052/055 classifications OR challenge WIN-054's ND classification — a rare two-sided process vulnerability. (3) A body/TLDR wording drift that is unlikely to mislead a careful reader but will produce friction for AI reviewers who audit against the TLDR definition.

Considering the other side: a dome defender's strongest play against this section is not to attack the taxonomy but to attack the empirical underpinning. 'Your criteria are fine in the abstract, but 0 of ${counts.total} is too clean — what are the conditions under which your review would find even ONE Confirmed WIN, and have you demonstrated those conditions are achievable in principle?' The section's 'no Confirmed category' paragraph answers the first half but not the second. The eg-falsifiability section partially covers this, so a one-sentence cross-reference would close it. This is a stronger-argument suggestion, not a hole.

A reasonable counterargument to my 'major' severity on the range-notation issue: this is a methodology section and the content of the argument (five tidal constituents, one geometric failure) is correct. A defender who pounces on '(045–051)' looks like they are avoiding the substantive point. However, credibility compounds: a range notation error in the section that explicitly sets out the review's classification rigor is more damaging than the same error elsewhere. Sustaining the 'major' severity.
